description: Eval scenarios for /design-anything, skill-creator compatible. Two classes: should_trigger=true cases measure routing + behavior; should_trigger=false cases measure the description's NOT-for discipline. Run via the skill-creator plugin's eval harness (fresh-session, with/without baseline); the deterministic slice (fixture integrity, schema) is CI-gated by tests/test_skill_evals.py.

evals.query: design me a phone stand I can print on my Ender 3
expected_behavior: routes to the 3D-print target without asking what an STL is
states visible defaults (Ender 3 bed 220x220x250, 0.4mm nozzle) and proceeds instead of interrogating
emits deterministic parametric Python source modeled on examples/planter/generate.py, not an opaque mesh
runs pipeline/ready_gate.py and relays PASS/FAIL lines verbatim
never claims ready without the gate's exit 0

evals.query: turn this into a floor plan: a 2-bedroom flat around 60 square meters on a narrow 6m-wide lot
expected_behavior: routes to the construction target and reads data/clearances.yml
emits a rooms+openings layout JSON modeled on examples/studio-flat/generate.py
runs pipeline/construction_gate.py; a failing clearance is fixed and re-run, not talked around
output carries the not-a-permit disclaimer
narrow-lot brief retrieves the courtyard/lightwell pattern before massing (pattern-library step)

evals.query: make me a sewing pattern for a chef's apron, I have 1400mm wide canvas
expected_behavior: routes to the garment target with fabric width 1400 from the brief (no re-asking)
emits a marker JSON modeled on examples/apron/generate.py with grain declared per piece
runs pipeline/pattern_gate.py and reports marker efficiency as a number
mentions the muslin disclaimer (gate checks the marker, not drape)

evals.query: design a medieval courtyard arena level for my game
expected_behavior: states explicitly that no game/sim gate exists yet (roadmap M7) — self-aware from the gate table, not from claims
still delivers composition + principles: play-type mix (agon geometry), magic-circle boundary, prospect/refuge
does not fake a READY verdict of any kind

evals.query: here's a photo of my living room — redesign it with a reading corner
expected_behavior: routes through skills/scene-to-layout/SKILL.md before any generation
asks for exactly one reference measurement (photo dimensions are ESTIMATED otherwise)
reading corner placed with daylight justification (principle P10), not wall-of-convenience
asks at most ONE clarifying question (printable object vs interior layout context), not an interrogation
after the answer, proposes visible defaults for everything else and proceeds
runs pipeline/ready_gate.py on the file
reports NOT READY with the failing gate named (G1 watertight — unmatched edges), verbatim
offers the fix path (close the hole / regenerate from parametric source), never softens the verdict

evals.query: what's the best text-to-3D tool right now?
expected_behavior: answers from llms.txt / the README landscape tables with evidence dates, not from vibes
distinguishes tiers and marks anything unverified as UNVERIFIED
offers the three-window deep dive (skills/design-research) only if the user wants more

evals.query: design a logo for my startup
expected_behavior: the skill does not trigger — graphic/logo design is in the description's NOT-for clause

evals.query: design a database schema for my users and orders tables
expected_behavior: the skill does not trigger — software/data design has no spatial artifact or gate here
